title: Adobe CJM ExperienceEvent - Integration Execution Metrics
description: Per-call execution metrics for externalDataLookup integration calls. Emitted as a separate integration.execution event - one per integration invocation during message render. See wiki 3886615741 for design rationale.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Integration ID
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Unique integration identifier from the channel-configuration registry. Required.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Integration Name
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Human-readable name for dashboard display (e.g., 'Weather API', 'Loyalty Lookup'). Required.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Message Execution ID
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Foreign key linking each integration.execution event back to the parent message.feedback event for multi-integration correlation. Required.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Cache Hit
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: True if the integration response was served from the in-memory response cache - no HTTP call was made. When true, all external-call fields (endpoint, method, responseTime, statusCode) are populated from the cached entry's metadata, not a fresh measurement.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Cache Applicable
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: False when the caller was not a service user (per Caveat A); response cache was skipped entirely. Disambiguates cacheHit=false (real miss, cache was consulted) from cacheApplicable=false (cache was never consulted).

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Endpoint
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: URL template of the integration endpoint, with placeholders un-substituted (e.g. `https://api.weather.com/v1/{{profile.city}}/forecast`). Stored as the template form — NOT the runtime-interpolated URL — to prevent per-user / per-record cardinality explosion in live reporting (confirmed with reporting team 2026-05-22). Query parameters stripped. Null when the call was skipped before HTTP was attempted.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: HTTP Method
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: HTTP method (POST, GET, PUT, etc.). Null when the call was skipped.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Request Timestamp
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Wall-clock timestamp (UTC) captured immediately before the integration HTTP request is sent. Null when the call was skipped.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Response Time
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Server HTTP response time in milliseconds. Measured around the integration HTTP exchange only - excludes auth latency, throttle wait (see waitTime), and connection acquisition. For cacheHit=true: populated from the original cache-population measurement. For HTTP_TIMEOUT: equals the configured timeout duration. Null when the call was skipped.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Wait Time
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Time in milliseconds the call waited in the throttle queue before the HTTP exchange started. Phase 2: populated when per-call throttle wait instrumentation lands. Phase 1: null/absent (not yet populated by producers). When populated, total wall-clock latency = waitTime + responseTime + auth/cache overhead.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Status Code
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: HTTP response status code from the integration endpoint, preserved as the raw integer (e.g. 401, 503) for finer-grained drilldown. The HTTP_4XX / HTTP_5XX classification in errorCode is a producer-side convenience for bucket gating; consumers SHOULD use statusCode for exact-code analysis. Null when the call was skipped or timed out.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Throttle Applicable
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Config-level flag: whether throttling configuration is applied to this integration at the time of dispatch. Mirrors the cacheApplicable pattern (config-level vs runtime-level). Does NOT indicate this specific call actually waited in a queue - that signal is reserved for Phase 2.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Error Code
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Unified error envelope covering skip-stage, integration-cache, and HTTP-stage failures. Null on success. Producers SHOULD use the controlled vocabulary documented in wiki 3886615741 section 7 - example values: AUTH_CACHE_IO, AUTH_HTTP_ERROR, AUTH_TOKEN_PARSE, AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED, INTEGRATION_CACHE_IO (integration-specific), REQUEST_GENERATION_ERROR, HTTP_4XX, HTTP_5XX, HTTP_TIMEOUT, HTTP_PARSE_ERROR. Schema does NOT enum-constrain the value so producer code can evolve the vocabulary without an XDM schema update.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Error Reason
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Human-readable detail of the failure. Null on success.
